params.label: Advanced Lateral Tuning
params.description: Advanced steering control changes to fine-tune how openpilot drives.

params.description: Actuator Delay

params.label: Force Auto-Tune On
params.description: Force-enable openpilot's live auto-tuning for "Friction" and "Lateral Acceleration".

params.label: Force Auto-Tune Off
params.description: Force-disable openpilot's live auto-tuning for "Friction" and "Lateral Acceleration" and use the set value instead.

params.label: Force Torque Controller
params.description: Use torque-based steering control instead of angle-based control for smoother lane keeping, especially in curves.

params.label: Always On Lateral
params.description: openpilot's steering remains active even when the accelerator or brake pedals are pressed.

params.label: Enable With Cruise Control
params.description: Enable "Always On Lateral" whenever "Cruise Control" is on, even when openpilot is not engaged.

params.label: Enable With LKAS
params.description: Enable "Always On Lateral" whenever "LKAS" is on, even when openpilot is not engaged.

params.label: Pause on Brake Press Below
params.description: Pause "Always On Lateral" below the set speed while the brake pedal is pressed.

params.label: Lane Changes
params.description: Allow openpilot to change lanes.

params.label: Automatic Lane Changes
params.description: When the turn signal is on, openpilot will automatically change lanes. No steering-wheel nudge required!

params.label: Lane Change Delay
params.description: Delay between turn signal activation and the start of an automatic lane change.

params.label: Minimum Lane Change Speed
params.description: Lowest speed at which openpilot will change lanes.

params.label: Minimum Lane Width
params.description: Prevent automatic lane changes into lanes narrower than the set width.

params.label: One Lane Change Per Signal
params.description: Limit automatic lane changes to one per turn-signal activation.

params.label: Lateral Tuning
params.description: Miscellaneous steering control changes to fine-tune how openpilot drives.

params.label: Force Turn Desires Below Lane Change Speed
params.description: While driving below the minimum lane change speed with an active turn signal, instruct openpilot to turn left/right.

params.label: Neural Network Feedforward (NNFF)
params.description: Twilsonco's "Neural Network FeedForward" model controller for smoother, model-based steering trained on your vehicle's data.

params.label: Smooth Curve Handling
params.description: Twilsonco's torque-based adjustments to smoothen out steering in curves.

params.label: Quality of Life
params.description: Steering control changes to fine-tune how openpilot drives.

params.label: Pause Steering Below
params.description: Pause steering below the set speed.

params.description: Advanced acceleration and braking control changes to

params.label: Show Speed Limit Sources

params.label: Advanced UI Controls
params.description: Advanced visual changes to fine-tune how the driving screen looks.

params.label: Hide Current Speed
params.description: Hide the current speed from the driving screen.

params.label: Hide Lead Marker
params.description: Hide the lead-vehicle marker from the driving screen.

params.label: Hide Map Settings Button
params.description: Hide the map settings button or map from the driving screen.

params.label: Hide Max Speed
params.description: Hide the max speed from the driving screen.

params.label: Hide Non-Critical Alerts
params.description: Hide non-critical alerts from the driving screen.

params.label: Hide Speed Limits
params.description: Hide posted speed limits from the driving screen.

params.label: Use Wheel Speed
params.description: Use the vehicle's wheel speed instead of the cluster speed. This is purely a visual change and doesn't impact how openpilot drives!

params.label: Developer UI
params.description: Detailed information about openpilot's internal operations.

params.label: Adjacent Path Metrics
params.description: Show the width of the adjacent lanes.

params.label: Developer Metrics
params.description: Performance data, sensor readings, and system metrics for debugging and optimizing openpilot.

params.label: Border Metrics
params.description:
Show statuses along the border of the driving screen.

Blind Spot: The border turns red when a vehicle is in a blind spot
Steering Torque: The border goes from green to red according to how much steering torque is being used
Turn Signal: The border flashes yellow when a turn signal is on

params.label: Lead Info
params.description: Show each tracked vehicle's distance and speed below its marker.

params.label: FPS Display
params.description: Show the frames per second (FPS) at the bottom of the driving screen.

params.label: Numerical Temperature Gauge
params.description: Show a numerical temperature in the sidebar instead of the status labels.

params.label: Use International System of Units
params.description: Display measurements using the "International System of Units" (SI) standard.

params.label: Developer Sidebar
params.description: Display debugging info and metrics in a dedicated sidebar on the right side of the screen.

params.label: Metric #1
params.description: Select the metric shown in the first "Developer Sidebar" widget.

params.label: Metric #2
params.description: Select the metric shown in the second "Developer Sidebar" widget.

params.label: Metric #3
params.description: Select the metric shown in the third "Developer Sidebar" widget.

params.label: Metric #4
params.description: Select the metric shown in the fourth "Developer Sidebar" widget.

params.label: Metric #5
params.description: Select the metric shown in the fifth "Developer Sidebar" widget.

params.label: Metric #6
params.description: Select the metric shown in the sixth "Developer Sidebar" widget.

params.label: Metric #7
params.description: Select the metric shown in the seventh "Developer Sidebar" widget.

params.label: Developer Widgets
params.description: Overlays for debugging visuals, internal states, and model predictions on the driving screen.

params.label: Adjacent Leads Tracking
params.description: Display adjacent leads detected by the car's radar to the left and right of the current driving path.

params.label: Model Stopping Point
params.description: Show a stop-sign marker where the model intends to stop.

params.label: Radar Tracks
params.description: Display all radar points produced by the car's radar.

params.label: Driving Screen Widgets
params.description: Custom FrogPilot widgets for the driving screen.

params.label: Acceleration Path
params.description: Color the driving path by planned acceleration and braking.

params.label: Adjacent Lanes
params.description: Show the driving paths for the left and right lanes.

params.label: Blind Spot Path
params.description: Show a red path when a vehicle is in that lane's blind spot.

params.label: Compass
params.description: Show the current driving direction with a simple on-screen compass.

params.label: Driving Personality Button
params.description: Control and view the current driving personality via a driving screen widget.

params.label: Gas / Brake Pedal Indicators
params.description:
On-screen gas and brake indicators.

Dynamic: Opacity changes according to how much openpilot is accelerating or braking
Static: Full when active, dim when not

params.label: Rotating Steering Wheel
params.description: Rotate the driving screen wheel with the physical steering wheel.

params.label: Model UI
params.description: Model visualizations for the driving path, lane lines, path edges, and road edges.

params.label: Dynamic Path Width
params.description:
Change the path width based on engagement.

Fully Engaged: 100%
Always On Lateral: 75%
Disengaged: 50%

params.label: Lane Lines Width
params.description:
Set the lane-line thickness.

Default matches the MUTCD lane-line width standard of 4 inches.

params.label: Path Edges Width
params.description:
Set the driving-path edge width that represents different driving modes and statuses.

Default is 20% of the total path width.

Color Guide:

- Blue: Navigation
- Light Blue: Always On Lateral
- Green: Default
- Orange: Experimental Mode
- Red: Traffic Mode
- Yellow: Conditional Experimental Mode overridden

params.label: Path Width
params.description:
Set the driving-path width.

Default (6.1 feet) matches the width of a 2019 Lexus ES 350.

params.label: Road Edges Width
params.description:
Set the road-edge thickness.

Default matches half of the MUTCD lane-line width standard of 4 inches.

params.label: \"Unlimited\" Road UI
params.description: Extend the length of the driving path, lane lines, and road edges for as far as the model can see.

params.label: Navigation Widgets
params.description: Map style, speed limits, and other navigation widgets.

params.label: Larger Map Display
params.description: Increase the map size for easier navigation readings.

params.label: Map Style
params.description:
Select the map style for "Navigate on openpilot" (NOO):

Stock openpilot: Default comma.ai style
FrogPilot: Official FrogPilot map style
Mapbox Streets: Standard street-focused view
Mapbox Outdoors: Emphasizes outdoor and terrain features
Mapbox Light: Minimalist, bright theme
Mapbox Dark: Minimalist, dark theme
Mapbox Navigation Day: Optimized for daytime navigation
Mapbox Navigation Night: Optimized for nighttime navigation
Mapbox Satellite: Satellite imagery only
Mapbox Satellite Streets: Hybrid satellite imagery with street labels
Mapbox Traffic Night: Dark theme emphasizing traffic conditions
Mike's Personalized Style: Customized hybrid satellite view

params.label: Road Name
params.description: Display the road name at the bottom of the driving screen using data from "OpenStreetMap (OSM)".

params.label: Show Speed Limits
params.description: Show speed limits in the top-left corner of the driving screen. Uses data from the car's dashboard (if supported) and "OpenStreetMap (OSM)".

params.label: Show Speed Limits from Mapbox
params.description: Use Mapbox speed-limit data when no other source is available.

params.label: Use Vienna-Style Speed Signs
params.description: Show Vienna-style (EU) speed-limit signs instead of MUTCD (US).

params.label: Quality of Life
params.description: Miscellaneous visual changes to fine-tune how the driving screen looks.

params.label: Show Driver Camera When In Reverse
params.description: Show the driver camera feed when the vehicle is in reverse.

params.label: Stopped Timer
params.description: Show a timer when stopped in place of the current speed to indicate how long the vehicle has been stopped.

params.label: Alert Volume Controller
params.description: Set how loud each type of openpilot alert is to keep routine prompts from becoming distracting.

params.label: Disengage Volume
params.description:
Set the volume for alerts when openpilot disengages.

Examples include: "Cruise Fault: Restart the Car", "Parking Brake Engaged", "Pedal Pressed".

params.label: Engage Volume
params.description: Set the volume for the chime when openpilot engages, such as after pressing the "RESUME" or "SET" steering wheel buttons.

params.label: Prompt Volume
params.description:
Set the volume for prompts that need attention.

Examples include: "Car Detected in Blindspot", "Steering Temporarily Unavailable", "Turn Exceeds Steering Limit".

params.label: Prompt Distracted Volume
params.description:
Set the volume for prompts when openpilot detects driver distraction or unresponsiveness.

Examples include: "Pay Attention", "Touch Steering Wheel".

params.label: Refuse Volume
params.description:
Set the volume for alerts when openpilot refuses to engage.

Examples include: "Brake Hold Active", "Door Open", "Seatbelt Unlatched".

params.label: Warning Soft Volume
params.description:
Set the volume for softer warnings about potential risks.

Examples include: "BRAKE! Risk of Collision", "Steering Temporarily Unavailable".

params.label: Warning Immediate Volume
params.description:
Set the volume for the loudest warnings that require urgent attention.

Examples include: "DISENGAGE IMMEDIATELY — Driver Distracted", "DISENGAGE IMMEDIATELY — Driver Unresponsive".

params.label: FrogPilot Alerts
params.description: Optional FrogPilot alerts that highlight driving events in a more noticeable way.

params.label: Goat Scream
params.description: Play the infamous "Goat Scream" when the steering controller reaches its limit. Based on the "Turn Exceeds Steering Limit" event.

params.label: Green Light Alert
params.description:
Play an alert when the model predicts a red light has turned green.

Disclaimer: openpilot does not explicitly detect traffic lights. This alert is based on end-to-end model predictions from camera input and may trigger even when the light has not changed.

params.label: Lead Departing Alert
params.description: Play an alert when the lead vehicle departs from a stop.

params.label: Loud \"Car Detected in Blindspot\" Alert
params.description: Play a louder alert if a vehicle is in the blind spot when attempting to change lanes. Based on the "Car Detected in Blindspot" event.

params.label: Speed Limit Changed Alert
params.description: Play an alert when the posted speed limit changes.

params.label: Use Pedal for Longitudinal Control
params.description: Use the pedal interceptor for longitudinal control instead of camera ACC/Redneck when available.

params.label: Smooth Pedal Response on Hills
params.description: Smoothen acceleration and braking when driving downhill/uphill.

params.label: Red Panda
params.description: Enable Red Panda behavior for GM (alternate safety config and bus numbering). Requires a reboot to take effect.

params.label: Remote Start Boots Comma
params.description: Use GM C9 SystemPowerMode for ignition detection. Toggle requires a panda firmware update and a reboot to take effect.

params.label: Stop-and-Go Hack
params.description: Force stop-and-go on the 2017 Chevy Volt.

params.label: \"Taco Bell Run\" Torque Hack
params.description: The steering torque hack from comma's 2022 "Taco Bell Run". Designed to increase steering torque at low speeds for left and right turns.

params.label: Dashboard Speed Offset
params.description: The speed offset openpilot uses to match the speed on the dashboard display.

params.label: Stop-and-Go Hack
params.description: Force stop-and-go on Lexus/Toyota vehicles without stock stop-and-go functionality.

params.label: Device Settings
params.description: Settings that control how the device runs, powers off, and manages driving data.

params.label: Device Shutdown Timer
params.description: Keep the device on for the set amount of time after a drive before it shuts down automatically.

params.label: Disable Logging
params.description:
WARNING: This will prevent your drives from being recorded and all data will be unobtainable!

Prevent the device from saving driving data.

params.label: Disable Uploads
params.description:
WARNING: This will prevent your drives from being uploaded to comma connect which will impact debugging and official support from comma!

Prevent the device from uploading driving data.

params.label: High-Quality Recording
params.description: Save drive footage in higher video quality.

params.label: Low-Voltage Cutoff
params.description: While parked, if the battery voltage falls below the set level, the device shuts down to prevent excessive battery drain.

params.label: Raise Temperature Limits
params.description:
WARNING: Running at higher temperatures may damage your device!

Allow the device to run at higher temperatures before throttling or shutting down. Use only if you understand the risks!

params.label: Use Konik Server
params.description: Upload driving data to "connect.konik.ai" instead of "connect.comma.ai".

params.label: Screen Settings
params.description: Settings that control screen brightness, screen recording, and timeout duration.

params.label: Screen Brightness (Offroad)
params.description: The screen brightness while not driving.

params.label: Screen Brightness (Onroad)
params.description: The screen brightness while driving.

params.label: Screen Recorder
params.description: Add a button to the driving screen to record the display.

params.label: Screen Timeout (Offroad)
params.description: How long the screen stays on after being tapped while not driving.

params.label: Screen Timeout (Onroad)
params.description: How long the screen stays on after being tapped while driving.

params.label: Standby Mode
params.description: Turn the screen off while driving and automatically wake it up for alerts or engagement state changes.

params.label: Automatically Download New Models
params.description: Automatically download new driving models as they become available.

params.label: Model Randomizer
params.description: Driving models are chosen at random each drive and feedback prompts are used to find the model that best suits your needs.

params.label: Recovery Power
params.description: Adjust the strength of planplus lane recovery corrections (0.5 to 2.0).

params.label: Stop Distance
params.description: Adjust the model's stopping distance in meters (minimum 4 for safety). Most users prefer 6.

params.label: Select Driving Model
params.description: Select the active driving model.
